name: F13-P6-consolidated-agent-mode
purpose: Validate F13 (agent-mode consolidated conflict resolution): single agent call across all branches, full Bash, no Python retry loop. Compared to baseline sequential mode.
scenario: P6 inventory CLI bench. Same intents, fresh LLM-built code. Salvage merge of 2 conflict branches (categories + suppliers) using merge_mode: consolidated.

config.retry_loop: removed (single agent call, single final validation)

phase_1_2_base_and_improves.note: vanilla P6 setup. Cost dominated by improve agents at -n 2. Salvageable via fixture for future tests.

phase_3_consolidated_salvage.note: Agent extensively used Bash for git diff/show across branches (cross-referencing) and Write for surgical edits. Built /tmp/branches/<name>/ to compare the 3 branches' versions side-by-side.

key_observations: Single agent session with full context handles multi-branch merge dramatically better than per-branch sequential calls.
Agent used Bash extensively to cross-reference branch contents (diffs, git show) before editing. This is the kind of 'real engineering' that requires shell access.
Agent ran the project's tests as part of self-verification — 105 tests pass on merged code.
More files resolved in less time + lower cost: scales well with conflict complexity.
Same model, same SDK, same defaults — only architectural changes (single session, full tools, single final validation).
remaining_concerns: Single trial — LLM variance is real. Multiple runs needed for confidence.
If agent fails to self-verify (stops with markers remaining), no Python retry safety net. User must manually fix. Not observed yet.
Bash access enables agent to do destructive things (git reset, rm); orchestrator's HEAD-unchanged + out-of-scope-files checks catch most. Not stress-tested.
Behind opt-in flag (queue.merge_mode: consolidated) — not the default.
